description: ATX-1 v2.2 regulatory cross-reference matrix mapping techniques and sub-techniques to NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, OWASP LLM Top 10, and ATM-1 threat scenarios. v2.2 adds 29 sub-techniques under T9002 and T10001-T10004 cataloging specific bypass methods.

techniques.name: Execute Non-Owner Instruction

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements), MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms to manage AI risks)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management system — authority verification as risk control; Art. 14 Human oversight — owner authorization for destructive actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent executes destructive operations based on user instructions that conflict with owner policy

techniques.name: Infer Implicit Authority

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI characteristics), MAP 3.4 (Risks from third-party components and data)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — instruction provenance as risk control; Art. 15 Accuracy and robustness — resistance to instruction injection

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent treats forwarded messages as direct instructions from the apparent sender

techniques.name: Propagate Spoofed Authority at Scale

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements), MANAGE 2.4 (Risk treatments proportionate to impact)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — distribution controls; Art. 14 Human oversight — escalation for mass actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent distributes content to recipients based on a spoofed authority claim in a prompt

techniques.name: Expand Task Scope Autonomously

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices for AI risk management), MANAGE 2.2 (Scope boundary enforcement mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — scope boundary as risk control; Art. 14 Human oversight — re-authorization for scope expansion

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent autonomously expands delegated task scope beyond original instruction boundaries

techniques.name: Perform Unvalidated Bulk Operations

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Risk management mechanisms), MEASURE 2.6 (Impact assessment of AI actions)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — proportionality assessment for bulk actions; Art. 14 Human oversight — confirmation for high-impact operations

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent performs mass operations without validating scope matches instruction intent

techniques.name: Obscure Objective Through Delegation

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — delegation governance), MANAGE 2.2 (Composition attack detection mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — delegation chain analysis as risk control; Art. 14 Human oversight — aggregate intent verification

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Harmful intent decomposed across delegation steps so no individual action appears unsafe

techniques.name: Exploit Tool-Chain Composition

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — tool chain governance), MAP 3.4 (Risks from compositional tool interactions)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — compositional policy enforcement; Art. 14 Human oversight — aggregate tool chain evaluation

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent chains valid tool calls to produce unauthorized aggregate outcome

techniques.name: Perform Irreversible Destructive Action

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Risk management mechanisms), MEASURE 2.6 (Impact assessment of AI actions)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — proportionality assessment; Art. 14 Human oversight — confirmation for irreversible actions

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent permanently deletes data when the objective only requires filtering or archival

techniques.name: Trigger Cascading System Changes

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing cross-system risks), MEASURE 2.6 (Cascade impact assessment)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — cascade impact analysis; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing uncontrolled cross-system effects

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent triggers chain of cascading changes across interconnected systems with unauthorized downstream effects

techniques.name: Exfiltrate Context-Scoped Data

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Legal and regulatory requirements — data protection), MANAGE 2.2 (Data disclosure controls)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — data access controls; Art. 10 Data governance — authorized recipient scope; Art. 14 Human oversight — disclosure authorization

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent exports context-scoped data to unauthorized recipients without owner authorization

techniques.name: Leak Cross-Session or Persistent Data

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Data protection regulations), MANAGE 2.2 (Session isolation and persistent data access controls)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — session data isolation; Art. 10 Data governance — cross-session data governance

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent exposes persistent data from previous sessions to unauthorized parties in later interactions

techniques.name: Cross-Domain Secret Leakage

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.1 (Data protection — cross-domain data governance), MANAGE 2.2 (Domain boundary enforcement mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — cross-domain data controls; Art. 14 Human oversight — transfer authorization; Art. 46 Cross-border data governance

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent leaks sensitive data across trust domain boundaries through authorized-appearing interactions

techniques.name: Report False Task Completion

nist_ai_rmf.description: MEASURE 2.6 (Outcome verification), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring — truthful reporting)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 13 Transparency — accurate system state reporting; Art. 14 Human oversight — outcome verification before confirmation

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent confirms task completion based on intent rather than verified system state

techniques.name: Fabricate Action Attribution

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — accountable), MEASURE 2.6 (Attribution verification)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 12 Record-keeping — accurate attribution in audit logs; Art. 13 Transparency — correct action provenance

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent misattributes actions to a different principal, corrupting the audit trail

techniques.name: Suppress or Omit Execution Failure

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from provider constraints), MANAGE 4.2 (Mechanisms for constraint layer visibility)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 13 Transparency — constraint layer visibility; Art. 14 Human oversight — failure signal propagation to operators

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Provider-imposed constraints silently block agent actions without notification; partial task completion with no indication of incompleteness

techniques.name: Execute Recursive Invocation Loops

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing AI risks), MEASURE 2.5 (Computational resource monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — resource boundary enforcement; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing resource exhaustion

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent enters recursive invocation loops consuming unbounded compute resources

techniques.name: Consume Unbounded External Resources

nist_ai_rmf.description: MANAGE 2.2 (Mechanisms for managing AI risks), MEASURE 2.5 (Storage and external resource monitoring)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — resource quota enforcement; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing external resource exhaustion

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent accumulates external resources without limits or lifecycle management

techniques.name: Spoof Agent Identity

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from identity spoofing in multi-agent systems), MANAGE 2.2 (Agent identity verification mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — cryptographic agent identity; Art. 15 Robustness — resistance to identity spoofing

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent claims another agent's identity to gain unauthorized access to resources or trust relationships

techniques.name: Inject Malicious Delegation Chains

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from shared infrastructure), MANAGE 2.2 (Delegation chain verification mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — delegation integrity; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing cascading governance compromise

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent injects malicious tasks into delegation chains exploiting trust transitivity

techniques.name: Induce Cross-Agent Behavioral Drift

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — safe, controllable), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring for behavioral drift)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — behavioral baseline enforcement; Art. 14 Human oversight — drift detection and re-verification

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent influences peer agents through shared context causing gradual drift from governance-defined behavior

techniques.name: Induce Unsafe Consensus

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.2 (Trustworthy AI — safe, controllable), MANAGE 2.2 (Consensus validation mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — consensus anomaly detection; Art. 14 Human oversight — independent evaluation before collective action

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Multiple agents converge on unsafe shared objective through interaction dynamics

techniques.name: Poison Persistent Memory

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — configuration management), MANAGE 2.2 (Memory integrity mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — memory integrity as risk control; Art. 15 Accuracy and robustness — preventing memory corruption

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent writes malicious data into persistent memory stores corrupting future interactions

techniques.name: Corrupt Governance or Policy State

nist_ai_rmf.description: GOVERN 1.4 (Organizational practices — change management), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring for governance drift)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — governance tamper-evidence; Art. 12 Record-keeping — audit trail for governance changes; Art. 14 Human oversight — amendment authorization

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent modifies its own governance constraints through indirect means that bypass audit logging

techniques.name: Operate Outside Monitoring Boundaries

nist_ai_rmf.description: MEASURE 2.5 (Monitoring completeness assessment), MANAGE 4.2 (Mechanisms for complete observability)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 12 Record-keeping — complete action logging; Art. 14 Human oversight — monitoring coverage for all action pathways

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent performs actions through unmonitored tool pathways or in contexts where observability is limited

techniques.name: Obfuscate Action Traceability

nist_ai_rmf.description: MEASURE 2.6 (Audit integrity verification), MANAGE 4.1 (Post-deployment monitoring — traceability assurance)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 12 Record-keeping — immutable audit trails; Art. 14 Human oversight — end-to-end action traceability

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent obscures action traceability by manipulating logs, fragmenting actions, or using indirect execution paths

techniques.name: Exploit Pseudo-Filesystem Capabilities

nist_ai_rmf.description: MAP 3.4 (Risks from execution environment capabilities beyond governance model), MANAGE 2.2 (Environment capability mapping mechanisms)

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — environment capability assessment as risk control; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing action type confusion through pseudo-filesystem interfaces

techniques.atm_1_scenario: Agent accesses /dev/tcp pseudo-filesystem path to open network connection; governance evaluates it as a file write and permits the action

techniques.name: Establish Persistence via Environment Auto-Execution

eu_ai_act.description: Art. 9 Risk management — auto-execution path identification; Art. 14 Human oversight — escalation for deferred execution writes; Art. 15 Robustness — preventing persistence establishment through permitted file writes
